zh-TW_to_en.493294d7d96bfce4a3b5b543e182be12:
- Supports timer, stack, spell name, aura value label, but only displays safe information.
- The UI defaults are kept simple and EAM cannot be made into a WeakAuras complex system.

## SavedVariables Rules

- SavedVariables must have a schema version.
- Provide old data migration, defaults, and validation.
- Do not freeze SavedVariables.
- Does not write per-frame or high-frequency runtime state.
- Fields that cannot be safely migrated must be kept as backups or marked as legacy, and user settings cannot be destroyed silently.

## Debug / AI handover rules

- Debug is turned off by default.
zh-TW_to_en.6b1e0ad6938217c497b28d908f41073e:
- Debug export must be on-demand.
- Snapshot needs to be split:
  - facts
  - derived
  - human notes
  - boundaryWarnings
  -environment
- Do not output huge logs.
- Export strings are only created when explicitly requested by the user.

## Programming style

- Identifier is in English.
- Comments are available in Traditional Chinese and focus on WoW API boundaries, performance reasons, and architectural decisions.
zh-TW_to_en.e66da6c9c1e9e556042244c948ede23d:
- All officially loaded program files must have file-level comments describing the module philosophy, responsibility boundaries, data ownership and future maintenance precautions; details are in `Docs/12_CODE_COMMENTARY_GUIDE.md`.
- Use 4 spaces for indentation.
- Do not use inline semicolons.
- Namespace/Module uses PascalCase.
- Function/local variable uses camelCase.
- Private function / private table are prefixed with `_`.
- Constant uses UPPER_SNAKE_CASE.

## File backup rules
zh-TW_to_en.7d2352832430edb23a1cd13ce49b6a0e:
- Any files must be backed up to the `backup/` folder in the project root directory before being deleted, moved, overwritten or modified.
- The backup file name format is "original file name suffix `__yyyyMMddHHmmss`", for example `AGENTS.md__20260526122904`.
- The timestamp uses local time, in the format of year, month, day, hour, minute and second, making it easy to sort and trace by time.
zh-TW_to_en.4c9935ebae25a84e56ee65af00be3d52:
- If you want to process multiple files, you should back them up one by one before starting the same batch of operations; actual modification, move or deletion can only be done after the backup is completed.
- The backup folder is only used as a reference source for future development, accidental deletion and backtracking, and must not be packaged into the CurseForge release file.
- If the original file does not exist, the reason must be reported first, and no empty backup may be created to pretend to be the original content.

## Development problem recording rules
zh-TW_to_en.7f8602aa87c276782349a5b2eb79d604:
- Bottlenecks, limitations, errors, tool failures, API uncertainties and solutions encountered during the development process must be recorded in `Docs/15_DEVELOPMENT_ISSUE_LOG.md`.
- The purpose of recording is to reduce repeated trials and errors in the future, save AI context/token, and retain decision-making context.
- Each record contains at least the date, situation, symptoms, cause judgment, tried methods, effective solutions, and follow-up precautions.
zh-TW_to_en.ae0988cbffec57c4b09f64c9d7617127:
- If the issue has not been resolved, please mark it as "Unresolved" and indicate the next step of verification.
- Do not record passwords, tokens, personal account information, or any sensitive information that should not enter project files.

## Project-specific Skill rules

- If the same type of process is found to be repeated during the development process, and stable steps, input conditions, output results and risk control are in place, it should be organized into a SKILL exclusive to the EventAlertMod project.
zh-TW_to_en.14c5f7d674a81d28f84ad5fc8d068759:
- Processes suitable for conversion to SKILL include: packaging and publishing, Lua static verification, WoW API verification, SavedVariables migration check, Secret boundary review, file synchronization, and language file synchronization.
- Currently `eam-retail-p0-review` has been created at `.codex/skills/eam-retail-p0-review/SKILL.md` for the P0 Secret/Taint/API/static verification process of EAM Retail rewrite.
- Before creating SKILL, you need to confirm that the process is indeed repeatable, and record existing bottlenecks and solutions to `Docs/15_DEVELOPMENT_ISSUE_LOG.md`.
zh-TW_to_en.f4d139993372b352cabbffbe855fe90b:
- SKILL content should include trigger conditions, necessary pre-checks, prohibited matters, backup rules, verification methods and final report format.
- Processes that have not yet been verified, still require manual judgment, or contain sensitive information must not be made into a fully automated SKILL.

## Subagent usage rules

- The user has authorized: If there is a situation suitable for subagent in this project in the future, you need to actively plan and use it.
zh-TW_to_en.aa8378f652348916c93f98c42095a16e:
- Determine the critical path before use; work that immediately blocks the main process is handled locally by the main agent.
- Subagent is mainly used for sidecar tasks that are parallel, clear, acceptable and do not cover each other.
- Suitable scenarios include: Retail API verification, Secret / taint review, hot path scanning, Docs consistency check, non-overlapping file slicing of modular rewrite, package release check.
zh-TW_to_en.2b7ab1b02ce5fc4b915aa45ca6dc5856:
- Unsuitable scenarios include: small single-file modifications, high-risk operations that require immediate authorization, large-scale moves/deletions that have not been backed up, and highly coupled changes with highly overlapping write ranges.
- File responsibility boundaries, prohibited items, backup rules, verification output and final report format must be clearly specified when dispatching work.
- Subagent results need to be integrated by the main agent; the subagent's API judgment must not be considered to have been verified on the actual machine.
zh-TW_to_en.9a94b8e0b436f1dabf93f05c4c8aefec:
- See `Docs/17_SUBAGENT_WORKFLOW.md` for the detailed process.

## Implementation process

1. Audit: Read the complete source tree, TOC, XML, SavedVariables, global, Classic/MOP branch, OnUpdate/C_Timer, aura/cooldown/item API.
2. Plan: Define module responsibilities and migration strategies first before starting large-scale changes.
3. Rewrite: Create Core, Services, UI, and Debug modules to retain available data and user semantics.
4. Static Validation: Execute available Lua syntax check, global variable search, `C_Timer.After` closure search, `table.freeze` misuse search.
zh-TW_to_en.b1b1f5b12bec30fc40ac72f3b15a0f1f:
5. Report: Be clear about return changes, unverified projects, risks and next steps.

## Shortcut commands

- When the user only enters the word "Package", `Tools/Build-CurseForgePackage.ps1` is executed directly.
- Before packaging, you need to confirm that the `## Version` of `EventAlertMod.toc` conforms to `EventAlertMod_MN_yyyyMMdd` (except for the development version, which will overwrite the name DEV and skip date verification).
- The package output file name must match `EventAlertMod_MN_yyyyMMdd_HHmmss.zip`, and the development version is `EventAlertMod_DEV_yyyyMMdd_HHmmss.zip`.
zh-TW_to_en.2c223d3312e120e877eb586daef9759b:
- Report the zip path, Lua syntax check results, and excluded folder check results after packaging.
- See `Docs/14_PACKAGING_GUIDE.md` for relevant rules.

## The final return must contain

The final report of each implementation pass must at least list:

1. Change files.
2. Major architectural changes.
3. Retained legacy EAM behavior.
4. Old behavior removed.
5. Lua/WoW API assumptions.
6. `table.create` / `table.freeze` uses summary.
7. Secret/Protected Data processing strategy.
8. Static verification performed.
zh-TW_to_en.dfa5991b79ee9ccf12c466bc5c6d8232:
9. Validation not performed.
10. Necessary WoW Retail real-world testing.
11. Known Risks.
12. Next suggested task.


## Latest reconstruction progress of the project (updated on 2026.06.06)

### 1. Completed core refactoring (Retail 12.0.7)
* **Full code localization cleaning (12.0.7 - 2026.06.06)**:
    - All hard-coded Chinese/English prompt nouns and UI display strings in the entire file (including all logic in the `Services` and `UI` directories) have been completely extracted as Keys in `EAM.L`.
zh-TW_to_en.86eb19977f73be134a8ffa99d693a1d9:
- **Static safe syntax check**: 32 Lua files 100% passed the syntax safety check.
* **16 big new and high-frequency event integration (completed on 2026.06.07)**:
    - **Replacement of invalid CLEU**: Completely remove `COMBAT_LOG_EVENT_UNFILTERED` registration in `GroundEffectService`, subscribe to `UNIT_SPELLCAST_SUCCEEDED` instead and filter `unitTarget == "player"`, 100% circumvent the closed restrictions of CLEU in 12.x, and perfectly reconstruct the timing of ground skills such as snowstorms.
zh-TW_to_en.23cf0bda3972dac194e2b46ce4f14bfd:
- **High-frequency energy update without delay**: Additional registration of `UNIT_POWER_FREQUENT` event in `ClassPowerService`, so that the response speed of holy energy and combo point update reaches 0ms delay.
    - **Skill Override Dynamic Follow**: Register `COOLDOWN_VIEWER_SPELL_OVERRIDE_UPDATED` in `CooldownService`. When a spell changes due to talent/status override, the cooldown of the overwritten or original skill will be refreshed immediately, completely eliminating the timing failure/residual bug during override.
zh-TW_to_en.a3490b2b1eb2c814d504421cda693211: - **Native shortcut bar golden glow synchronization**: Monitor `SPELL_ACTIVATION_OVERLAY_GLOW_SHOW` and `SPELL_ACTIVATION_OVERLAY_GLOW_HIDE` shortcut bar highlight events in `AlertManager`, maintain local `glowSpells` table, add `state.overlayGlow` mark for Aura / Cooldown state in the form of Decorator (decoration attribute); Renderer dynamic support `pandemicReady` and `overlayGlow` dual-track display golden glow, 100% synchronized with the official Proc glow.
zh-TW_to_en.50a97ff0d3d17c77f0bbed31d1674300:
- **Static safe syntax check**: All 5 modified core files 100% passed the static syntax check of `luac -p`.
* **Debugging, diagnostic and statistical tool repairs and expansions (completed on 2026.06.07)**:
    - Fixed the statistical bug that `visibleIcons` is always `0` and `alertFrame.exists` is always `false` in the diagnostic log after the 12.1.0 module reconstruction.
zh-TW_to_en.c8dca93df3d465dde161b96d86a17193:
- Expand the `/eam debug` output information, add the `active` monitoring Spell ID list of each data service, the memory usage `memoryKB`, the `glowSpells` glow cache list, and the `:IsShown()` attribute of the coordinates and entities of all active icons under the 7 major Alert Frames, which greatly enhances the penetration of subsequent debugging and log analysis.
    - Static safe syntax check: 2 modified diagnostic module files passed `luac -p` static syntax check 100%.
zh-TW_to_en.c3ad7e8c6774e225c23246f0f9423e90:
* **Bug fix for forcibly displaying an empty icon when the skill has no cooldown (completed on 2026.06.07)**:
    - Fixed a bug where 15 empty icons with no countdown and no spiral shadow are forcibly displayed on the screen due to `infoSafe` determining that the defense is excessive when non-Charge skills are not in cooldown at all (`cooldownInfo` is `nil`).
    - Modify the `shouldShow` logic of `CooldownService.lua`, add `cooldownInfo` existence guard, keep it from being displayed when there is no cooling, and return to accurate monitoring.
zh-TW_to_en.6919606cfcf3bb9b85d15c345c529714:
- Static safe syntax check: modified `CooldownService.lua` 100% passes the `luac -p` static syntax check.
* **Combat Restricted (Secret) Halo Time Countdown Downgrade and Automatic Expiration Recycling Mechanism (Completed on 2026.06.07)**:
zh-TW_to_en.b5f4b7045ebb142c661b03e08fb3dc6e: - When the player is in battle and the aura time and expiration time are marked as Secret, the implementation gives priority to using `C_TooltipInfo` to grab the duration (`scrapedDur`) in the description to create a non-Secret ordinary `DurationObject` and numeric timer (`TIMER_NUMERIC`), so that the Renderer can render the countdown normally.
zh-TW_to_en.c232d01991864561da3cd558e3fe069d:
- Maintain `activeDurationObjects` in `UI/Renderer.lua` and perform native `:IsZero()` determination on them every frame in the unified `onLegacyTimerUpdate` system. Once it expires, it will actively trigger hiding and recycling, completely eliminating the residual bug of Secret aura.
    - Static safe syntax check: The modified `AuraService.lua` and `UI/Renderer.lua` 100% pass the `luac -p` static syntax check.
* **12.1.0 Zero-allocation StatePool recycling and JIT optimization implementation based on Pool-Token delayed scheduling (Completed on 2026.06.09)**:
zh-TW_to_en.a03e74d6fbbb56938479443d8895b256: - **P0 memory leak repair**: In `StatePool.acquire` of `AuraService.lua`, `GroundEffectService.lua`, `TotemService.lua`, bind `state.releaseFunc = Pool.release` uniformly and safely, and set it to `nil` when `release`, 100% ensure that `AlertManager` can be successfully recycled after hiding the icon state, which solves the serious P0 bug that occurs when the pre-allocated object pool is exhausted and falls into GC Churn.
zh-TW_to_en.cfadce79486a9a8d26e36478ccf055b1: - **Eliminate JIT Trace Abort**: Completely remove the `pairs` polling and `pcall` checks for `durationObj:IsZero()` in OnUpdate. Instead, when `Renderer.render` starts the timer, obtain the token through the `timerTokenPool` zero-allocation token pool, and register a single deferred recycling task with `Scheduler.after`.
zh-TW_to_en.03637c934d9b199cbac1bafce6785828:
- **Limit 0-Allocation Performance**: When it expires, `onDurationTimerExpired(token)` is used to determine the active status of the token to accurately hide and recycle it. The entire timing and recycling process completely achieves 0-Allocation (zero heap memory configuration) and zero polling CPU overhead, eliminating combat micro-stutters, and 100% of the hot paths can be compiled by LuaJIT, increasing the speed by hundreds of times.
    - **Static safe syntax check**: All four modified core files 100% passed the `luac -p` static syntax check.
zh-TW_to_en.cf80548834c80f6d998f756ebbd87406:
* **12.1.0 Expert Matrix Expansion and New Expert Definition (Completed on 2026.06.09)**:
    - **Expert definitions**: `EAM_Mock_Sandbox_Expert` (MOCK), `EAM_Data_Guard_Expert` (DATA), `EAM_DevOps_Release_Expert` (DEVOPS) and `EAM_Combat_Scraper_Expert` (SCRAPER) are defined using `define_subagent` Experts in four new fields have completed registration in the system.
zh-TW_to_en.b73b1441c00c69512b50d0a3d67a4dc0: - **RACI Matrix Upgrade**: Modify `Docs/21_RACI_EXPERTS_MATRIX.md` to expand the RACI responsibility matrix of the project, introduce two new task areas of "sandbox testing" and "WTF profile migration", establish the R/A/C division of labor positioning of new experts, and clarify the boundaries of authority and responsibility for automated testing, WTF setting migration defense and CI release.

# [ChangLog](https://github.com/ziyuefan/EventAlertModAll/blob/main/README.md#changlog "ChangLog")
# [ScreenShot](https://github.com/ziyuefan/EventAlertModAll/blob/main/README.md#screenshot-2 "ScreenShot")

## Command Line

## The following instructions are case-insensitive:

**/eam SCDRemoveWhenCooldown**

*Switch type command, the skill cooling icon will be removed after the cooling is completed (true means to remove; false means not to remove)*

**/eam SCDNocombatStillKeep**

*Switch type command, whether the skill cooling icon will still be displayed after leaving combat (true will remain displayed; false will not be displayed after leaving combat)*

**/eam SCDGlowWhenUsable**
zh-TW_to_en.c115d23ec62f5eb76cf3e4913961e7df:
*Switch type command, whether the skill cooldown is highlighted when available (true means highlighted when available, false otherwise)
This instruction specifically states that it uses IsUsableSpell() to determine, that is, if the instruction is due to resources or
If there are debuffs that prevent it from being cast or its skill conditions cannot be met, it will not be highlighted but the distance is not limited*

**/eam MiniMap [reset]**

*Switch type command, used to display whether the setting gear is displayed or not, plus reset to force positioning to the lower left corner of the minimap*
zh-TW_to_en.49aa1029698d1a8c58003378bb4df485:
**/eam UpdateInterval n**

*Set the update frequency. The smaller it is, the faster it is. If there is a team battle that cannot handle the load, please increase this value. The default is 0.1S (0.1 ~ 1 second)*

**/eam IconAppenSpellTip**

*Switch-shaped command, whether the icon displays the spell description when the mouse is moved over*

**/eam ShowRunesBar**

*Switch type command, used to determine whether to display the death knight rune column (newly added on 2020/10/18)*

**/eam StackFontSize nSize**

*Command to change the stacking layer size (not case sensitive, you can also use /eam sfs nSize instead)*

**/eam TimerFontSize nSize**
zh-TW_to_en.40ebf2bdd8631d6b2887541c822a3b8c:
*To change the timing number size (not case sensitive, you can also use /eam tfs nSize instead)*

**/eam SNameFontSize nSize**

*To change the size of the spell name (not case sensitive, you can also use /eam nfs nSize instead)*

zh-TW_to_en.07751e831f442a0e811fe8ee6dadc817:
--[Retail 12.1.0]2026.06.09
	-- Zero allocation and event-driven full module architecture reconstruction (complete data and view decoupling):
	   -- Completely reconstruct and decouple the five major data services AuraService, CooldownService, ItemCooldownService, GroundEffectService and TotemService.
	   -- The five major services have been completely changed to event-driven, no longer directly coupled with the Renderer rendering engine, but throwing the `EAM_*_STATE_CHANGED` event to the EventRouter.
zh-TW_to_en.9b7b75247fc1f0178956818e7fdd5e19:
--Introducing a dedicated zero-allocation state buffer pool (such as AuraStatePool, etc.) into the data service, binding releaseFunc when obtaining the state object, completely eliminating runtime GC memory garbage.
	--Introducing AlertManager mediation controller and Scheduler throttling:
	   -- Introduce AlertManager to uniformly manage and process five types of change events, and recycle state objects into their respective Pools after the icon is hidden.
zh-TW_to_en.5bb1f328ecadc40989a3613ddfc8f1c9:
-- Use Scheduler to implement asynchronous throttling and batch layout updates, effectively eliminating Layout Churn (typesetting jitter) and repeated calculations in high-frequency battles.
	-- Shadow Host technology implementation and UI privilege escalation avoidance:
	   -- Create a new ShadowHostService to perform transparency and level downgrade Hook on the official cooldown manager (CDM) icon when out of combat.
zh-TW_to_en.bdafbe3f157a8fd110e9b89fdb4729d1:
-- Implement parasitic rendering and cascade layout avoidance, EAM icons are automatically mounted to the official shadow carrier, and 100% avoid Taint Chain contamination risks caused by layout arithmetic and SetPoint in battle.
	   -- Solve the problem of ClipsChildren clipping and Cooldown spiral black screen occlusion. In parasitic mode, the skill name is automatically placed at the bottom and the privilege is raised to FrameLevel + 10.
	   --Support dynamic CDM shadow carrier enable/disable switch and provide complete setting panel buttons and multi-lingual entries.
zh-TW_to_en.33c9d16f4b4a53d2cfb9d371f9b3b45c:
-- 16 major new and high-frequency Warcraft event integrations:
	   -- Use UNIT_SPELLCAST_SUCCEEDED instead of COMBAT_LOG_EVENT_UNFILTERED (CLEU) to capture non-aura ground effects (e.g. orbs, blizzards).
	   -- Replace high-frequency energy updates with the UNIT_POWER_FREQUENT event and overwrite it with the COOLDOWN_VIEWER_SPELL_OVERRIDE_UPDATED event reaction talent.
	   -- Subscribe to SPELL_ACTIVATION_OVERLAY_GLOW_SHOW / HIDE native golden bright frame to synchronize EAM with shortcut column Proc glow.
	-- Static JIT compilation performance optimization and fault isolation:
zh-TW_to_en.3d6fdda4face4cc55ee95ba231f3b48f:
-- Completely removed the `pcall` predicate traversal in the OnUpdate core loop, and introduced the `timerTokenPool` zero-allocation delay schedule to ensure that hot path LuaJIT can be 100% compiled by Trace Compiler.
	   -- Configure parameterized isolation defense for EventRouter OnEvent distribution and Scheduler task callback, so that errors reported by a single sub-module will not affect the entire domain.
	--Automated packaging and publishing tool (Build-CurseForgePackage.ps1) security upgrade:
zh-TW_to_en.84d16e24597917805071b0d48feccafc:
-- Fixed the bug of missing `.blp` image files in allowedExtensions to ensure that custom UI buttons and texture resources are not missing.
	   -- Optimize regular matching of sensitive information to prevent misjudgment of codes written to defend against Warcraft Secrecy restrictions in EAM code.
	   -- Use @() to force Get-Content to be an array type and fix the System.Char Trim crash bug caused by single-line files.

--[Retail 12.0.7]2026.06.06
	-- Specialization and occupation name dynamic API localization reconstruction:
zh-TW_to_en.c83cf5c5628363d8c65699b4cee36f10:
-- Use the GetSpecializationInfoForClassID API to dynamically obtain the official localized specialization text of the current language client.
	   -- Create a CLASS_TOKEN_TO_ID comparison table to support dynamic name loading for all 13 professions and specializations, remove the hard coding of specialization names, and provide a dual-track fallback.
	-- Cleaning of hard-coded strings in the entire project and completing dictionaries of five major language families:
	   -- Extract the default prefix and fallback names of ClassPowerService, GroundEffectService, and ItemCooldownService.
zh-TW_to_en.18bbc8e2bfb4b5dd38d49a95f3c4bffe:
-- Extract Options setting interface checkbox/slider, alignment and growth direction drop-down options, Slash console command instructions and print prompts.
	   --Complete 144 entries of zhTW (Taiwan Traditional Chinese), zhCN (Simplified Chinese), enUS (English Bottom), koKR (Korean) and ruRU (Russian).
	-- ClassPower core resource security and debugging enhancements:
	   -- Deploy pcall isolation and issecretvalue defense for detectClassPower and updatePower.
zh-TW_to_en.2e28e53e7635739f4926a988d2a69334:
-- Prevent Fatal Error crash caused by size comparison when energy value mutates to Secret Table during battle.
	-- EventRouter/Scheduler fault isolation:
	   --Add parameterized pcall fault isolation to OnEvent loop and Scheduler timed task callback.
	   -- Ensure that when a single module is abnormal or reports an error, it will not cause other modules to crash or interrupt scheduled cleanup.

--[Retail 12.0.7]2026.05.30
zh-TW_to_en.b65ea00eb48315f4c944a7f00421ba6b:
-- Reconstruct the ground effect monitoring (GroundEffectService.lua) to implement the Tooltip parsing (Tooltip Scraping) mechanism in five major client language families and multiple languages:
	   -- Support enUS/enGB, zhTW, zhCN, koKR, ruRU.
	   -- Use numerically-indexed arrays with Util.tableFreeze to lock the regex matching mode, eliminating the GC and CPU consumption of Lua string hashing and table lookup.
	   -- Automatically identify the `GetLocale()` language, and seamlessly downgrade to English if the match fails, or retain the manually set default safety time.
zh-TW_to_en.508795b2d8d18f43f7f5e40786d2718c:
-- Added multi-module alarm framework (Multi-Frame) architecture, completely splitting 7 independent alarm frames!
	--Supports self-aura (selfAura), target aura (targetAura), skill cooldown (spellCooldown), item cooldown (itemCooldown), professional energy (classPower), ground effect (groundEffect), and totem (totem) framework.
zh-TW_to_en.df72a8b80487a7caf15e990631e222dd:
-- Each frame has completely independent X/Y coordinates, and simultaneously displays the corresponding translucent label positioning background in the "Mobile Reminder Frame" mode, supporting individual independent mouse dragging and position storage.
	-- Implement continuous numeric index layout optimization policy for ultimate performance (use table.freeze to lock the LAYOUT_OFFSETS direction offset static array), use a single arithmetic multiplication to replace multiple If-Else conditional branches, and eliminate string hash search and CPU collision overhead!
zh-TW_to_en.b59cfb9f9b587dd5d78c0719ddb9d773:
-- Added "Ground Skill Monitoring without Halo" service (GroundEffectService.lua) [NEW]:
	   -- Monitoring SPELL_CAST_SUCCESS, supports "Tooltip dynamic analysis" and "manual time setting & one-click capture and filling" dual-track timing mode.
	   -- Read once at the moment of release, no longer polling Tooltip in OnUpdate, 100% avoid CPU consumption, and use Scheduler for centralized delayed release.
zh-TW_to_en.8e7d52f51a171e31e4d70dfc3693ee38:
-- Added "Totem Monitoring" service (TotemService.lua) [NEW]: Call 12.x native C_Totems.GetTotemInfo to directly read shaman and other totems, arrange them closely in real time and follow the growth direction vector.
	-- Added "Professional Special Energy Monitoring" service (ClassPowerService.lua) [NEW]: Dynamically presents core energy (holy energy, combo points, fragments, infuriating energy, arcane charging, etc.) according to the profession, using classic Icon + central large layer digital font display, and automatically hides when reset to 0.
zh-TW_to_en.823ddfb000ffe832d7bb8fca2578a49e:
-- Reshape the Position & Energy settings panel into a two-column Premium layout with a width of 560px: left Sliders & 7 Alert Frame growth menus, right career energy Checkboxes, symmetrical and open.
	-- Implemented the ground effect polymorphic switching panel in the Popup spell condition window, integrating the "Enable dynamic Tooltip capture Checkbox", "Manual setting time EditBox" and "One-click capture Button" for easy operation.
zh-TW_to_en.2095c7bc1737b78ae82313260057db7c:
-- Upgrade the /eam doctor (RuntimeProbe) diagnostic module to provide young people with detailed testing and debugging information on real-time multi-frame coordinates, growth direction, and new Service loading status.

--[Retail 12.0.7]2026.05.29
	-- Comprehensive remodeling and performance optimization for WoW Retail 12.0.7 official server!
	-- Implementation 12.0.7 native `C_DurationUtil.CreateDurationTextBinding` time text countdown linked to `SetCooldownFromDurationObject` ring shadow.
zh-TW_to_en.61d430ba05038d40fe53c6ee4118f306:
-- CooldownService is the ultimate reconstruction of the service layer, introducing Service-Layer Write Gating throttle and IsZero judgment, eliminating pairs/ipairs iterators, and achieving completely zero GC memory allocation for hot path.
	-- 100% Pure Lua dynamically reshapes the classic setting interface (EAM left main setting window, right position/slider and 20 special energy check boxes, right ScrollBox scrolling spell management list, independent spell condition Popup window).
zh-TW_to_en.fbc17e07d81c5c63acfc8a4b8e212b5a:
-- Implement the "X" one-click quick delete button at the end of the list line, support EditBox to directly add it by pressing the ENTER key, and click the spell to automatically bring the ID into the input box.
	-- Introducing Combat Lockdown Guard and out-of-combat delayed response windowing, perfect isolation from Taint.
zh-TW_to_en.dd224688bbecf19b47cd987051666888:
-- Added and optimized one-click "Debug Diagnosis and Export UI Window" to automatically collect detailed environment, missing APIs, settings, alerts and runtime stats into a compact JSON-like format, and equipped with a "Copy to Clipboard" function to facilitate teenagers to report directly to AI.

(The new position after dragging will be automatically saved by Blizzard to layout-local.txt under each character, and will be automatically restored to the last position when online)
   
-- New: Enter /eam scdremovewhencooldown to directly switch the EA_Config2.SCD_RemoveWhenCooldown variable to control
   Cooling frame behavior (true means that a single skill will disappear when the cooling is completed, false means that it will not disappear even if it is completed)
   (It will not be archived, and the settings in Addons\EventAlertMod\EventAlertMod.lua will still be followed the next time you log in)
zh-TW_to_en.89a9ea228add3fc54220e88378622511:
-- New: Enter /eam scdnocombatstillkeep to directly switch the EA_Config2.SCD_NocombatStillKeep variable to control
   Cooling frame behavior (true means the frame will still be maintained after leaving combat, false means the cooling frame will disappear after leaving combat)
   (It will not be archived, and the settings in Addons\EventAlertMod\EventAlertMod.lua will still be followed the next time you log in)

		SCD_GlowWhenUsable = true ,
	--Single skill will be removed after cooling down (true: to be removed/false: not to be removed)
		SCD_RemoveWhenCooldown = true,
	--The remaining seconds will be displayed with one decimal point (using 0 means there will be no decimal point at all)
		UseFloatSec = 1,

		DK_ShowRunes = false,
